mechanism_narrative: Sustained industrial production growth signals rising capacity utilization and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter. A three-month moving average of INDPRO above its six-month trend is a reliable leading indicator of equity upside.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO 60-day forward return prediction falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the signal produces negative Sharpe ratio when traded as a long-only strategy.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 5% signals the onset of labor market weakness, reducing consumer income and spending, which directly lowers corporate profits and equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: After UNRATE crosses 5%, the 20-day forward S&P 500 return is positive more than 60% of the time, or the average return is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is low but inflation remains elevated, real interest rates are deeply negative, which erodes the discount rate advantage of equities and signals a mispricing that eventually corrects lower. The joint condition of FEDFUNDS below 2% and CPIAUCSL above 3% YoY is negative for forward returns.
failure_condition: In periods when FEDFUNDS < 2% and CPIAUCSL > 3% YoY, the 20-day forward return is positive in more than 55% of observations, or the interaction term has zero incremental predictive power in a linear regression.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary easing cycle defined by a federal funds rate below 1.5%, strong GDP growth is amplified by cheap financing and boosts equity valuations, making quarterly GDP increases a positive signal for forward returns.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 1.5%, the rank correlation between GDPC1 and forward 60-day S&P 500 return is below 0.10, or the conditional return is not significantly positive at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in retail sales (RSAFS) of more than 2% signals an immediate consumer spending drop, which directly impacts corporate revenues and triggers short-term negative equity returns within the next week.
failure_condition: After a >2% monthly decline in RSAFS, the 5-day forward S&P 500 return is positive more than 50% of occurrences, or the average return is not statistically different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls indicate labor market strength, higher disposable income, and robust corporate earnings, providing a persistent tailwind for equity prices over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The monthly change in PAYEMS has a rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year window, or the signal fails to produce a positive mean return when traded as a long-only strategy on month-ahead forwards.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of rising housing starts with a low federal funds rate signals robust construction activity and favorable financing conditions, jointly driving positive equity returns over a longer horizon as housing investment multiplies through the economy.
failure_condition: The interaction term of HOUST growth and FEDFUNDS level has zero incremental predictive power for 60-day forward returns in a regression controlling for each variable individually, or the conditional Sharpe ratio is negative over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation exceeds 3%, it triggers expectations of monetary tightening, raising the equity risk premium and compressing valuations, leading to negative short-term equity returns within the following week.
failure_condition: After CPI YoY > 3%, the 5-day forward S&P 500 return is positive more than 50% of the time, or the average return is not significantly negative at the 5% level.
